questions.question: World cancer day is observed on _______?
questions.explanation: Each year on 4 February, WHO and International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) supports Union for International Cancer Control (UICC) to promote ways to ease the global burden of cancer, which is observed as World Cancer Day.

questions.question: 15th January is celebrated as ______?
questions.explanation: Army Day is celebrated on 15 January every year in India, in recognition of Lieutenant General K. M. Cariappa's taking over as the first Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army from General Sir Francis Butcher, the last British Commander-in-Chief of India, on 15 January 1949.

questions.question: Zero Discrimination Day was first celebrated in the year _______?
questions.explanation: Zero Discrimination Day is an annual day celebrated by the UN and other international organisations. The day aims to promote equality before the law and in practice throughout all of the member countries of the UN. The day was first celebrated on March 1, 2014.

questions.question: July 1st is celebrated in India in honour of which professionals?
questions.explanation: In India the National Doctors' Day is celebrated on July 1 all across India to honour the legendary physician and the second Chief Minister of West Bengal, Dr Bidhan Chandra Roy.

questions.question: 3rd May is celebrated as ________?
questions.explanation: World Press Freedom Day was proclaimed by the UN General Assembly in December 1993, following the recommendation of UNESCO's General Conference. Since then, 3 May, the anniversary of the Declaration of Windhoek is celebrated worldwide as World Press Freedom Day.

questions.question: National statistics day is observed in memory of ____________?
questions.explanation: In recognition of the notable contributions made by Late Professor Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis in the fields of statistics, statistical system and economic planning, Government of India in 2007, designated 29th June the birth anniversary of Prof. Mahalanobis. as the "Statistics Day" in the category of Special Days to be celebrated every year at the National level.

questions.question: When is Hiroshima Day observed?
questions.explanation: During the final stage of World War II, the United States dropped nuclear weapons on the Japanese city of Hiroshima on August 6. It is observed as Hiroshima Day.

questions.question: July 26th is celebrated by India for its victory in which among the following battles/wars?
questions.explanation: Kargil Vijay Diwas is celebrated on 26 July every year in honour of the Kargil War's Heroes. This day is celebrated in the Kargil - Dras sector and the national capital New Delhi, where the Prime Minister of India, pays homage to the soldiers at Amar Jawan Jyoti at India Gate every year.

questions.question: October 5 is celebrated as ____________?
questions.explanation: World Teachers' Day, also known as International Teachers Day, is held annually on October 5 since 1994, commemorates teacher organizations worldwide.

questions.question: Human Rights day is observed every year on 10th of which month?
questions.explanation: Human Rights Day is observed every year on 10 December. It commemorates the day on which, in 1948, the United Nations General Assembly adopted the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

questions.question: Kisan Divas is observed in remembrance of who among the following personalities?
questions.explanation: Birth anniversary of Ch. Charan Singh is on December 23, Saturday. It is observed as Kisan Divas or Farmers day. Since, farmers are the backbone of an economy therefore our nation celebrates Farmers Day in the memory of late Ch. Charan Singh, the ex-Prime Minister of India. He was primarily a farmer himself.

questions.question: UNICEF Day is celebrated on __________?
questions.explanation: On December 11, the anniversary of the creation of UNICEF is remembered. Since its founding, the United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund provides humanitarian and developmental assistance to children and mothers in developing country.

questions.question: World Radio day was established by _______?
questions.explanation: On 14 January 2013, the United Nations General Assembly formally endorsed UNESCO's proclamation of World Radio Day. During its 67th Session, the UN General Assembly endorsed the resolution adopted during the 36th session of the UNESCO General Conference, proclaiming 13 February, the day United Nations Radio was established in 1946, as World Radio Day.

questions.question: National Integration Day is celebrated on __________?
questions.explanation: National Integration Day is observed every year on 19th of November all over the India. It is celebrated as the birthday anniversary of the first woman prime minister of the India Indira Gandhi.

questions.question: Hindi Diwas is celebrated on __________?
questions.explanation: Hindi Day is celebrated every year on September 14. On September 14, 1949, the Constituent Assembly decided by one vote that Hindi was the official language of India.

questions.question: World Population Day falls on _________?
questions.explanation: World Population day is an annual event, observed on July 11 every year, which seeks to raise awareness of global population issues.

questions.question: June 1st is celebrated as __________?
questions.explanation: World Milk Day is a day established by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations to recognise the importance of milk as a global food. It has been observed on June 1 each year since 2001.

questions.question: World book day was first celebrated in the year ________?
questions.explanation: World Book Day is a yearly event celebrated on April 23rd, organized by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO), to promote reading, publishing and copyright. World Book Day was celebrated for the first time on 23 April 1995.

questions.question: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated every year on 24th of which month?
questions.explanation: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated by Ministry of Panchayati Raj on 24 April annually. Then Prime Minister of India Manmohan Singh inaugurated the first National Panchayati Raj Day in 2010.

questions.question: March 9th is celebrated in connection to which among the following armed forces of India?
questions.explanation: In India 9th March of every year is observed as Central Industrial Security Force Raising Day. It was on this day in 1969 that CISF came into force.

questions.question: Feast of Saint Valentine is celebrated throughout the world on __________?
questions.explanation: Valentine's Day, also called Saint Valentine's Day or the Feast of Saint Valentine, is an annual holiday celebrated on February 14.

questions.question: Which among the following day celebrates the date on which the Constitution of India came into effect?
questions.explanation: Republic Day honours the date on which the Constitution of India came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: World Consumer Day is observed on _________?
questions.explanation: "World Day of Consumer Rights" which is celebrated each year worldwide was adopted by the United Nations on the 15 of March 1985. It was celebrated for the first time on 15 March 1983, since then it became an important opportunity to activate citizen role.

questions.question: 28th February is celebrated in India as _________?
questions.explanation: National Science Day is celebrated in India on 28 February each year to mark the discovery of the Raman effect by Indian physicist Sir Chandrashekhara Venkata Raman on 28 February 1928.

questions.question: Pravasi Bharathiya Divas is observed on _________?
questions.explanation: Pravasi Bharatiya Divas is celebrated in India on 9 January every year to mark the contribution of the overseas Indian community to the development of India.

questions.question: Which among the following day falls in the month of January?
questions.explanation: The National Voters' Day is celebrated every year to mark the foundation day of the Election Commission of India, which was established on 25th January, 1950.

questions.question: World Day for International Justice is observed on ___________?
questions.explanation: World Day for International Justice recognizes the nascent and strengthening system of international justice that has emerged since the Nuremburg and Tokyo Trials after World War II. It is observed on July 17th.

questions.question: International Literacy Day is celebrated every year on 8th of which month?
questions.explanation: International Literacy Day, celebrated annually on 8 September, is an opportunity for Governments, civil society and stakeholders to highlight improvements in world literacy rates, and reflect on the world's remaining literacy challenges. The issue of literacy is a key component of the UN's Sustainable Development Goals and the UN's 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

questions.question: World habitat day is observed every year on first ________ of October.
questions.explanation: The United Nations designated the first Monday of October of every year as World Habitat Day to reflect on the state of our towns and cities, and on the basic right of all to adequate shelter. It is also intended to remind the world that we all have the power and the responsibility to shape the future of our cities and towns.

questions.question: December 1 is observed as _____________?
questions.explanation: World AIDS Day is held on the 1st December each year and is an opportunity for people worldwide to unite in the fight against HIV, show their support for people living with HIV and to commemorate people who have died. World AIDS Day was the first ever global health day, held for the first time in 1988.
